proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib=1 yields a higher objective_response rate than treatment_osimertinib=0 (canonical EGFR TKI pairing).
Among patients with kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 yields a higher objective_response rate than treatment_sotorasib=0.
Among patients with brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib=1 yields a higher objective_response rate than treatment_olaparib=0.
Among patients with tmb_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 yields a higher objective_response rate than treatment_pembrolizumab=0.

analyses.code: chi2 test ORR by osimertinib within egfr_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: ORR 16.4% on osimertinib (n=1973) vs 16.6% off (n=4544); RD=-0.002, p=0.87 — NOT significant, no benefit detected.

analyses.code: chi2 test ORR by sotorasib within kras_g12c==1
analyses.result_summary: ORR 17.5% on sotorasib (n=2246) vs 16.6% off (n=4122); RD=0.008, p=0.43 — NOT significant.

analyses.code: chi2 test ORR by olaparib within brca2_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: ORR 14.6% on olaparib (n=417) vs 16.8% off (n=1102); RD=-0.022, p=0.35 — NOT significant.

analyses.code: chi2 test ORR by pembrolizumab within tmb_high==1
analyses.result_summary: ORR 19.7% on pembrolizumab (n=7111) vs 16.7% off (n=7147); RD=+0.030, p=5.4e-06 — SIGNIFICANT. Pembrolizumab benefits TMB-high patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 increases objective_response within the subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 relative to pembrolizumab=0; the benefit is concentrated in PD-L1 high.
Among pdl1_tps<0.01 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 confers no benefit on objective_response.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2: pembro vs not within pdl1_tps>=0.5
analyses.result_summary: ORR 20.7% on pembro (n=5682) vs 15.5% off (n=5730); RD=+0.052, p=9.3e-13 — large SIGNIFICANT positive interaction.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2: pembro vs not within pdl1_tps<0.01
analyses.result_summary: ORR 16.9% on pembro (n=2856) vs 17.2% off (n=2935); RD=-0.002, p=0.85 — no benefit in PD-L1 negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab confers a higher objective_response rate among smoking_status='current' or 'former' than among smoking_status='never'.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by smoking_status
analyses.result_summary: Pembro RD: current smokers +0.012 (p=0.048), former +0.011 (p=0.017), never-smokers +0.001 (p=0.91). Effect concentrated in ever-smokers; no benefit in never-smokers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is similar in adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by histology
analyses.result_summary: Adenocarcinoma RD=+0.009, p=0.030. Squamous RD=+0.013, p=0.044. Both subgroups show small significant benefits, no strong heterogeneity by histology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response in stk11_mutation=0 patients but not in stk11_mutation=1 patients (STK11 is a known immunotherapy resistance marker).

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by stk11_mutation; logistic with interaction.
analyses.result_summary: STK11 wild-type: pembro RD=+0.014, p=9.7e-05 (significant). STK11 mutant: pembro RD=-0.015, p=0.084 (trend toward harm). Logistic interaction coef=-0.20, p=3.0e-03 — STK11 negates pembrolizumab benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: keap1_mutation=1 attenuates the pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response, similar to STK11.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by keap1_mutation; logistic with interaction.
analyses.result_summary: KEAP1 WT: pembro RD=+0.011, p=2.4e-03. KEAP1 mut: pembro RD=+0.003, p=0.73. Interaction term coef=-0.046, p=0.47 — direction consistent but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is larger in sex_female=1 than sex_female=0.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by sex_female; logistic interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Females: pembro RD=+0.021, p=2.6e-05 (significant). Males: pembro RD=+0.000, p=0.94 (null). Logistic interaction coef=+0.145, p=2.6e-03 — pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in women.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit declines as ecog_ps increases; in ecog_ps>=2 there is no benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by ecog_ps level.
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: pembro RD=+0.016, p=0.010. ECOG 1: RD=+0.009, p=0.046. ECOG 2: RD=+0.001, p=0.91. Benefit concentrated in fit patients (ECOG 0-1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is larger in stage_iv=0 than stage_iv=1 (less aggressive disease responds more).
Pembrolizumab effect on objective_response is unchanged by has_brain_mets and liver_mets.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by stage_iv.
analyses.result_summary: Stage IV: pembro RD=+0.013, p=1.4e-03 (significant). Non-stage-IV: RD=+0.004, p=0.47. Counter to hypothesis: benefit is significant in stage IV; the smaller non-stage-IV cohort has higher baseline ORR (~19.6%).

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 by has_brain_mets and by liver_mets.
analyses.result_summary: Brain mets: RD=+0.005, p=0.65 in mets+, RD=+0.011, p=2.0e-03 in mets-. Liver mets: RD=+0.007, p=0.43 vs RD=+0.010, p=4.6e-03. No statistically significant interactions; modest dilution in metastatic-organ subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) modifies pembrolizumab effect on objective_response (e.g., reduced benefit in elderly).

analyses.code: Logistic regression with age_years × treatment_pembrolizumab interaction adjusted for ECOG/stage/albumin.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.0008 per year, p=0.74. No significant age modification of pembrolizumab effect across age bands <55, 55-64, 65-74, >=75.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the composite favorable subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND stk11_mutation=0 AND smoking_status!='never', treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response by >5 percentage points.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 within composite biomarker-favorable subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 22.6% on pembrolizumab (n=3886) vs 15.8% off (n=3927); RD=+0.068, p=2.3e-14. Largest pembrolizumab effect identified — concentrated in PD-L1 high, STK11-wt, ever-smokers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative prognostic main effect).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of ecog_ps in responders vs non-responders.
analyses.result_summary: Mean ECOG: responders 0.660 vs non-responders 0.827; diff=-0.167, p=7.9e-95. Strongest single ORR predictor.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of weight_loss_pct_6mo by responder status.
analyses.result_summary: Mean weight loss: 3.38% (R) vs 3.93% (NR); diff=-0.55, p=1.2e-32. Larger weight loss strongly predicts non-response.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of albumin_g_dl by responder status.
analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin: 3.82 (R) vs 3.80 (NR); diff=+0.023, p=1.4e-04. Significant but small magnitude.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of crp_mg_l by responder status.
analyses.result_summary: Mean CRP: 5.78 (R) vs 6.15 (NR); diff=-0.37, p=6.8e-04. Higher CRP associates with non-response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv=1 reduces objective_response.
has_brain_mets=1 reduces objective_response.
tmb_high=1 increases objective_response (main effect).
sex_female=1 increases objective_response (main effect).

analyses.code: chi2 ORR by stage_iv.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 15.4% in stage IV (n=32365) vs 19.6% in non-stage IV; RD=-0.042, p=1.1e-33. Strong negative prognostic effect.

analyses.code: chi2 ORR by has_brain_mets.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 14.4% with brain mets (n=12584) vs 17.7% without; RD=-0.034, p=2.0e-18. Brain mets strongly predict lower ORR.

analyses.code: chi2 ORR by tmb_high.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 18.2% TMB-high (n=14258) vs 16.4% otherwise; RD=+0.018, p=1.0e-06. Modest positive main effect.

analyses.code: chi2 ORR by sex_female.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 17.5% female (n=22371) vs 16.4% male; RD=+0.012, p=5.9e-04. Small positive main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: race_ethnicity is associated with objective_response, with white patients showing the lowest ORR.
insurance_type is associated with objective_response, with medicaid/uninsured patients showing lower ORR than private insurance.

analyses.code: Chi-square race x ORR; multivariable logistic adjusting for ECOG/stage/albumin/PDL1/TMB/pembro.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: white 16.5%, hispanic 17.2%, black 17.8%, asian 17.7%, other 18.5%; chi-sq p=0.045 (borderline). After adjustment, no race coefficient remains significant (smallest adjusted p≈0.11). Effect appears confounded by clinical features.

analyses.code: Chi-square insurance x ORR; multivariable logistic adjusting for ECOG/stage/albumin.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ORR: medicaid 16.1%, medicare 16.8%, private 17.2%, uninsured 17.0%; overall chi-sq p=0.23 — NOT significant. After adjustment, private vs medicaid coef=+0.087, p=0.022 (modest).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricted to KRAS_G12C=1 AND ecog_ps<=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 raises objective_response (since fitter patients are most likely to respond).

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 within kras_g12c==1 & ecog_ps<=1.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 18.9% on sotorasib (n=1891) vs 17.9% off (n=3523); RD=+0.010, p=0.41. Still NOT significant even after restricting to performance-fit KRAS G12C patients. Sotorasib appears inert in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricted to EGFR_mutation=1 AND ecog_ps<=1, treatment_osimertinib=1 raises objective_response.
Restricted to EGFR_mutation=1 AND smoking_status='never' (a classic EGFR TKI-favorable subgroup), osimertinib=1 raises ORR.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 within egfr_mutation==1 & ecog_ps<=1.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 17.4% on osimertinib (n=1682) vs 17.7% off (n=3844); RD=-0.003, p=0.78. NOT significant — no osimertinib benefit even in fit EGFR+ patients.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 within egfr_mutation==1 & smoking_status=='never'.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 15.3% on osimertinib (n=1153) vs 16.5% off (n=2672); RD=-0.012, p=0.36. NOT significant; osimertinib shows no benefit in EGFR+ never-smokers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricted to BRCA2_mutation=1 AND ecog_ps<=1, treatment_olaparib=1 raises objective_response.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 within brca2_mutation==1 & ecog_ps<=1.
analyses.result_summary: ORR 14.8% on olaparib (n=364) vs 17.5% off (n=961); RD=-0.027, p=0.29. NOT significant; trend toward worse ORR. Olaparib appears inert/neutral in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with rare biomarkers (braf_v600e, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, her2_amplification, msi_high), at least one of the four study treatments produces a detectable objective_response increase.

analyses.code: For each rare biomarker subgroup, chi2 ORR by each of the 4 treatments.
analyses.result_summary: Across braf_v600e (n=995), met_ex14 (n=1501), ret_fusion (n=1018), ros1_fusion (n=468), her2_amp (n=1467), msi_high (n=487), and four treatments each: only one nominally significant signal — osimertinib in BRAF V600E shows lower ORR (14.7% vs 20.5%, p=0.04), likely chance among ~24 tests. No targeted-therapy-by-rare-biomarker matching benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 23 SNP markers (snp_rs*) is significantly associated with objective_response after Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.05/23≈0.0022).

analyses.code: Univariate t-tests of each SNP genotype across responders vs non-responders.
analyses.result_summary: Lowest p-values: snp_rs7412 diff=+0.008, p=0.018; snp_rs1050828 diff=-0.007, p=0.031; remaining 21 SNPs p>0.25. None survive Bonferroni correction (threshold ~0.0022). SNPs do not meaningfully predict ORR.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model of objective_response, ecog_ps, stage_iv, weight_loss_pct_6mo, has_brain_mets, tmb_high, albumin_g_dl, crp_mg_l, sex_female, treatment_pembrolizumab, and pdl1_tps remain independently associated; the other three treatments do not.

analyses.code: Statsmodels Logit on objective_response with main-effect features (demographics, key biomarkers, lab/clinical, treatments).
analyses.result_summary: Independent predictors (p<0.01): ecog_ps coef=-0.382 (p=1e-95), stage_iv -0.303 (p=1e-34), weight_loss -0.039 (p=2e-33), has_brain_mets -0.260 (p=4e-19), tmb_high +0.129 (p=2e-06), albumin +0.093 (p=1e-04), crp_mg_l -0.005 (p=5e-04), sex_female +0.081 (p=8e-04), treatment_pembrolizumab +0.076 (p=1.6e-03), pdl1_tps +0.173 (p=2e-03). treatment_sotorasib (p=0.85), treatment_olaparib (p=0.65), treatment_osimertinib (p=0.18) NOT significant. EGFR/KRAS/STK11/KEAP1/TP53 main effects not significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an interaction-augmented logistic model, the pembrolizumab × pdl1_high (>=0.5) interaction term is large and positive.
The pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction term is negative.
The pembrolizumab × sex_female interaction term is positive.

analyses.code: Logit with treatment_pembrolizumab × (pdl1_tps>=0.5) interaction adjusted for clinical covariates.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.378, p=5.7e-11. Strongly positive — confirms PD-L1 high is the dominant pembrolizumab effect modifier.

analyses.code: Logit with treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.202, p=3.0e-03. Negative — STK11 mutation abolishes pembrolizumab benefit.

analyses.code: Logit with treatment_pembrolizumab × sex_female interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.145, p=2.6e-03. Positive — pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in women.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three-way structure pembrolizumab × pdl1_high × stk11_mutation shows that the pembrolizumab benefit is present ONLY in pdl1_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0; in stk11=1 even PD-L1-high patients gain no benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified chi2 of pembrolizumab effect within 4 PD-L1 × STK11 cells.
analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 hi & STK11 wt: pembro RD=+0.064, p=5.6e-15 (strong benefit). PD-L1 hi & STK11 mut: RD=-0.005, p=0.86 (no benefit). PD-L1 lo & STK11 wt: RD=+0.001, p=0.87 (no benefit). PD-L1 lo & STK11 mut: RD=-0.018, p=0.07 (trend to harm). Confirms three-way interaction: pembrolizumab works essentially only in PD-L1-high STK11-wt patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cardiac/respiratory/metabolic comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, autoimmune_disease, depression_anxiety_diagnosis, interstitial_lung_disease_history) are independently associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Univariate chi2 for each comorbidity.
analyses.result_summary: All 10 comorbidities tested have p>0.05 except coronary_artery_disease (RD=-0.009, p=0.051, borderline). Comorbidities have no meaningful effect on ORR in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient-reported symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of symptom grades by responder status.
analyses.result_summary: Only fatigue_grade is borderline (R 1.32 vs NR 1.35, diff=-0.027, p=0.04). pain_nrs (p=0.78), dyspnea_grade (p=0.49), cough_grade (p=0.26), appetite_loss_grade (p=0.35) all null. Symptoms add little after controlling for ECOG.

proposed_hypotheses.text: SUMMARY HYPOTHESIS: In this cohort, only one of the four treatments (treatment_pembrolizumab) has a real effect on objective_response, and that effect operates through the canonical immunotherapy effect modifiers (pdl1_tps>=0.5, ever-smoker, stk11_mutation=0, sex_female). The three targeted therapies (osimertinib in EGFR, sotorasib in KRAS_G12C, olaparib in BRCA2) show no benefit even within their canonical biomarker subgroups.

analyses.code: Synthesis: combine main-effect logistic, interaction tests, and biomarker-stratified chi-squares.
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab adjusted main-effect OR≈1.08 (p=1.6e-03); within PD-L1 high + STK11 wt + smoker, ORR rises 22.6% vs 15.8% (RD=+0.068). Sotorasib in KRAS+: p=0.43 (null). Osimertinib in EGFR+: p=0.87 (null). Olaparib in BRCA2+: p=0.35 (null). The dataset reproduces the canonical ICI biomarker pattern (PD-L1, STK11, smoking, sex) but does NOT reproduce TKI/PARP biomarker-driven activity in their nominal indications.
